spec_match.rationale: Thoroughly demonstrates using the content property's slash alt-text segment with counter() across different dynamic visual states (done, active, pending).

interactive.rationale: Highly interactive with custom controls that update the step states dynamically and update the a11y readout in real-time.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Framing is spot-on, contrasting the old ARIA duplication pattern with the new dynamic render-time evaluation.

edge_cases.rationale: Uses a robust stylesheet insertion try-catch to detect browser-level support for counters inside the content alt-text slot.

references.rationale: Fails to include the mandatory chromestatus.com/feature/<id> reference link anywhere on the page.

design_system.rationale: Perfect alignment with repository CSS styling conventions, typography, and spacing variables.

openQuestions.title: Add mandatory chromestatus.com spec reference link
openQuestions.detail: The page is missing the mandatory reference link to the ChromeStatus entry. Please add a 'see also' or 'references' list containing a link to `https://chromestatus.com/feature/5185442420621312`.

summary: A high-quality, extremely interactive concept demo showing step progress trackers with custom visual-to-spoken states driven by CSS counters. However, it fails the mandatory references check by completely omitting the ChromeStatus entry link.
